title: test_shutdown_stubborn_child_gets_sigkill flakes due to pgrep cross-contamination
description:
tests/integration/test_shutdown.py::test_shutdown_stubborn_child_gets_sigkill uses _no_orphans() which calls pgrep -f fake_claude.py. This is a system-wide match that picks up fake_claude.py subprocesses still being reaped from the previous test (test_shutdown_all_tasks_back_to_open also uses scenario=slow with 10s sleep). The test passes in isolation but fails intermittently in the full suite.

Reproduction: just test (run multiple times; 1-in-N runs fails).

Fix ideas:
- Scope pgrep to the supervisor's PGID or tag fake_claude with an env var (e.g. FAKE_CLAUDE_TEST_ID) and grep for the tag.
- Or wait_for_orphans_to_die(timeout=2s) before asserting.
- Or run integration tests serially within a process group/cgroup.
close_reason: Scoped _no_orphans() to per-test tag (str(tmp_path)) with 2s poll; closed TaskRunner cancel/spawn race in runner.py. 50/50 stress runs pass.

title: lets change fleet to log attempts into .claude/tasks/<task_id>
close_reason: moved per-attempt logs and failures counter into .claude/tasks/<task_id>/; API now takes artifact_dir directly; all 215 tests pass

title: lets add functinoality that fleet in its task folder in .claude/tasks/<task_id> also creates PLAN_AND_STATUS.md, KNOWLEDGE.md and lets add instructions into CLAUDE and AGENTS instructions to always populate and keep those files up date and check before starting new task as this can be task continuation
close_reason: Fleet now pre-creates PLAN_AND_STATUS.md and KNOWLEDGE.md stubs in each task's artifact dir; prompts/CLAUDE.md and prompts/AGENTS.md updated to instruct agents to read these on every start (task may be a continuation) and keep them up to date; README artifacts table and adapter prompt string updated; 190/190 unit tests passing.

title: test_dynamic_config_new_cap_respected_after_completion is timing-brittle, fails locally
description:
tests/integration/test_dynamic_config.py::test_dynamic_config_new_cap_respected_after_completion fails consistently on local machine.

Root cause: timing margins too tight. Test waits for 4 in-flight (~3-4s elapsed since t-000 started), writes config, sleeps 2s + 6s = 8s, then asserts in_flight <= 2. Total elapsed when assertion fires: ~11-12s since t-000 started. Slow scenario sleeps 10s per task and tasks are claimed ~1s apart, so only t-000 reliably completes by assertion time, leaving 3 in-flight (assertion expects <= 2).

Test comment says '~7s already elapsed' when _wait_in_flight returns, but observed elapsed is ~3-4s.

Production code (Supervisor / SpawnController) is functioning correctly: cap IS respected (no new spawns observed after t-000 completes). This is a test-only issue.

Suggested fix: extend the post-config sleep from 6.0s to ~10.0s, or reduce FAKE_CLAUDE_SLEEP_SEC for the 'slow' scenario.

Repro:
  uv run pytest tests/integration/test_dynamic_config.py::test_dynamic_config_new_cap_respected_after_completion

Logs from failing run show only t-000 completed before assertion at 11:22:12; other 3 tasks still in-flight.

Note: test file is currently UNTRACKED (uncommitted).
close_reason: Test fixed: replaced brittle sleep(6.0) with deterministic in_flight<=2 polling + 2s stable window. 3/3 stability runs pass at ~14.4s each.

title: Please validate that README file is in proper shape
close_reason: Validated README; fixed log layout table and retry-exhausted FAQ; doc tests still 13/13 pass

title: Please validate that just test is green

comments.author: Sergii Makarevych
comments.text:
Validation result: `just test` is NOT green.

Summary: 205 passed, 1 failed in 95.96s.

Failing test: tests/integration/test_dynamic_config.py::test_dynamic_config_new_cap_respected_after_completion

This test is an UNTRACKED file (not yet committed). The failure is a timing-margin issue in the test, not a production bug — the supervisor correctly respects the new max_concurrent cap (no spawns observed after a task completes when in_flight > new cap). Filed as fleet-4dz.

title: /sddw:requirements fllet_config we want to be able to change config when fleet is running and fleet should take this into account - maybe re-reading config every time it checks for tasks ?
notes: Requirements spec written to .sddw/fleet_config_dynamic_reload/requirements.md — 15 FRs, full acceptance criteria, in/out-of-scope, prohibitions, testing approach. Behavior is grounded in existing implementation (src/fleet/config.py, src/fleet/supervisor.py:_config_poll_loop) and existing integration tests (tests/integration/test_dynamic_config.py, tests/test_cli_config.py).
